zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde於大約1170年出生在英國爾海德。她於1187年與Sir Gilbert de la Hyde在英國波克夏結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde于大约1170年出生在英国爾海德。她于1187年与Sir Gilbert de la Hyde在英国波克夏结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Hyde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of various places so called in Bedfordshire, Cheshire, Hampshire, and Middlesex. They were named with Old English hīd ‘hide (of land)’, a variable measure of land, differing from place to place and time to time, and seems to have been originally fixed as the amount necessary to support one (extended) family (Old English hīgan, hīwan ‘household’). The surname may also be topographic for someone living on (and farming) a hide of land. The Hyde family has been in Leinster, Ireland, since the early 13th century and one family has been established in the county of Cork since the 16th century. The name was Gaelicized as both Dalaithíd and de hÍde. Compare Hyder .

variants.name: Dollarhide
meanings: Irish: possibly an altered form of de la Hyde (see Hyde ), which was a prominent surname in Leinster from medieval times onward. Compare Dollahite .

History: Francis Dollahide, born c. 1650–60 in County Dublin, Ireland, was the ancestor of Americans with the surname Dollahide, Dollahite, and Dollarhide. In 1680 he was transported to MD for some youthful misdemeanor. He appears to have been an educated man and rose to be an important landowner and politician, serving in the Lower House of the MD legislature as an elected representative for Baltimore County from c. 1705 to 1721.

variants.name: Hyder
meanings: English (Kent and Sussex): status name or topographic name for one who lived on or possessed a hide of land, from Middle English hide + the locative suffix -er. This is a characteristically southeastern English name, synonymous with Middle English atte hide elsewhere (see Hyde ).

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde was born about 1170, in Hyde, Cheshire, England. She married Sir Gilbert de la Hyde in 1187, in Berkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde est née environ 1170, à Hyde, Cheshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir Gilbert de la Hyde en 1187, à Berkshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde wurde um 1170 in Hyde, Cheshire, England geboren. Sie hat 1187 in Berkshire, England Sir Gilbert de la Hyde geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde è nata il circa 1170 nel Hyde, Cheshire, England. Lei sposato Sir Gilbert de la Hyde nel 1187, nel Berkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Hyde, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアハイド
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hydeは約1170年にイギリスチェシュアイアハイドで生まれた。1187年にイギリスバークシュアイアでSir Gilbert de la Hydeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde(은)는 대략 1170년 영국 체실르 히드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1187년 영국 버크실르에서 Sir Gilbert de la Hyde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde nasceu aproximadamente 1170, em Hyde, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Sir Gilbert de la Hyde em 1187, em Berkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde родилась около 1170 г. в местности хид, чешир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Gilbert de la Hyde в 1187 г. в местности биркшир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Gilbert de la Hyde nació aproximadamente 1170, en Hyde, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Sir Gilbert de la Hyde en 1187, en Berkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
